findings.evidence: PR line 129 proves `(isFG R C).ContainsZero`; Mathlib `CategoryTheory/ObjectProperty/ContainsZero.lean:101` has `instance [P.ContainsZero] : HasZeroObject P.FullSubcategory`, and `FGComoduleCat` is `(ComoduleCat.isFG R C).FullSubcategory` in `Finite.lean:64`.
findings.fix: Remove this instance and rely on the general instance after `ComoduleCat.isFG_containsZero`; keep the named `FGComoduleCat.zero` and `isZero_zero` lemmas if desired.
findings.issue: `FGComoduleCat.hasZeroObject` is a special-case duplicate of the generic `ObjectProperty.FullSubcategory` zero-object instance, so it adds an unnecessary overlapping typeclass path.

summary: The ambient zero-comodule API is at a natural level, but the finite-generation zero-object instance duplicates Mathlib's general full-subcategory instance once `ContainsZero` is proved.
